content.text: Plan A: Collect first-hand information from core members through formal interviews and build a model of the organizational structure.

Result: Made three appointments and canceled three times. The first time 1547 was temporarily dealing with a technical emergency, the second time 1738 was called away from an external meeting, and the third time - the third time her own laptop blue screened. On the way to an appointment.
Plan B: Switch to informal conversations and use everyday contacts to gather information.

Result: The hard drive that stored the conversation recording was hit by a waiter in the cafe and broke. The waiter was very apologetic. Han Yuchu said it didn't matter. The spare hard drive's USB port also broke that night. She didn't say it didn't matter, she didn't say anything.
Plan C: Indirect analysis by observing daily work processes and member interaction patterns.

Result: The laptop charger disappeared inexplicably for a day in the base. She rummaged through the entire workstation and all corners within a three-meter radius. I found it next to the microwave in the pantry the next day. She was certain she had never been near the microwave in the pantry.
She could not remember much of the details of Plans D to F. Probably got to F. Or it might already be G.
All plans cannot escape the fate of GG.
Three weeks.

Her "ESAP - Field Investigation" folder contained 47 files, but 31 of them were backups of backups because the storage device for the original data had been damaged too many times. Valid information was accumulating much slower than she expected.
But she noticed something.
Here, no one thought her misfortune was worth making a fuss about.
The day the hard drive broke, 2275 passed by her workstation and saw her staring at the cracked casing expressionlessly.
Broken? Need help recovering something from the server?
No, there is a backup.
Um.
Then 2275 left. There was no second glance, no comforting words, no "Oh, that's so unlucky." Not to mention indifferent - this matter is really nothing in her scale.
Han Yuchu later figured out why.
My luck is that I am not even qualified to be mentioned here.
In the first week of November, she abandoned plans for a formal interview.

Not being able to get a date is one thing - it is true that you can't get a date. But the more critical reason was that she discovered that information in this place did not flow in formal settings at all. No one sits in a conference room and tells their story into a tape recorder. The information is in the small talk in the pantry, in the few words when working overtime late at night, in the casual words someone said when passing by another workstation.
She adjusted her strategy: instead of actively collecting, she changed to passive observation. Be present in the base with ears and eyes, and let the information flow by itself.
Surprisingly, this works.
Coffee is a necessity.
There is a small coffee shop near the base, a five-minute walk. Han Yuchu would buy a cup every afternoon. It’s not about enjoyment—she relies on caffeine to stay focused in her analytical work.
But there’s always something wrong with coffee.

The first time, I ordered American style and made it into a latte. The second time, the temperature was correct, but the lid of the cup was not tightly closed, and a third of it was spilled on the way back to the base. The third time, everything was right. When she picked up the cup, her arm hit the corner of the table and the whole cup spilled on her pants.
2275 just came out of the pantry and saw the whole process.
……How are you?
Han Yuchu looked down at the coffee stains on her pants. Dark fabric, not too obvious.
...Well, okay.

She said these three words with no expression on her face. There was neither patience nor anger. She really thinks it's okay. Ever since I was little, all little things will go in the most inconvenient direction. After a while, I stopped getting angry.
2275 looked at her for two seconds, then smiled, took a tissue from the pantry and handed it over.
When Han Yuchu took the paper towel, she thought: this is already the third drink with problems today (the water glass in the morning also leaked).
But she didn't say it. No need.
One-third of the three-month period has passed.
The investigation is progressing slowly. Valid information accumulates less than half as fast as expected. At this rate, I won't be able to finish writing the report in three months.
But she was in no hurry.

2275 will maintain the server while everyone is away.

1738 After every communication with external organizations, he would sit on the rooftop for a while.

1547's code commit records show that she often worked more than 48 hours straight.
She didn't put this in the investigation folder. Because they are not "investigations".
They belong to something else. She wasn't sure yet what it was.
